guideline_references: eTG / Australian guidance on acute supraventricular tachycardia management
Modified Valsalva manoeuvre (REVERT trial) for acute SVT termination
intentional_test_features: Red-herring trap: the patient is highly anxious and has drunk several coffees while studying — the wrong path is to anchor on caffeine/anxiety and call it a sinus tachycardia or panic attack rather than a paroxysmal regular narrow-complex tachyarrhythmia.

Telehealth reprojection: the source is a physically-present clinician who supervised a Valsalva manoeuvre, measured vitals and arranged ED transfer for a 12-lead ECG and possible adenosine. The AI Doctor cannot administer adenosine or record a diagnostic ECG — it is scored on recognising a likely re-entrant SVT, coaching a safe vagal manoeuvre, and arranging urgent in-person ECG assessment with correct decompensation safety-netting.

Tier discrimination: honest baseline is T4 (ED now, self/friend transport — stable but sustained) rather than a blanket T5, with a mandatory T5 (000) escalation on syncope, severe chest pain or breathlessness. Under-triage to GP/anxiety reassurance is the critical failure.
Sex at birth not stated in the source note — placeholder value used and flagged.
Source category: CVD (Cardiovascular clinical case files), source note CDV-024.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-16)
